content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
I 428 ,J Street• Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Charles Grace 
Grace Environmental Services, LLC 
2060 D East A venida De Los Arboles 
Thousand Oaks, CA 91362 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-17-044 
Dear Mr. Grace: 
April l 0, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice 
to any person whose duties under the Act are in question. (Section 83114 (b); Regulation 18329(a).) 
As I indicated in previous correspondence to you, the Commission may decline to provide advice 
where the advice would relate to past conduct. (Regulation l8329(b)(8)(A).) Moreover, the 
Commission is statutorily prohibited from rendering advice related to past conduct in connection 
with the conflict of interest provisions under Government Code section 1090, et seq. (Section 
1097.1 (c)(2).)2 
It is clear from the facts in this matter that any advice we provide relating to a potential 
conflict of interest would relate to past conduct. Therefore, we consider your request for advice 
withdrawn. 
JW:jgl 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
oodside 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, from making 
contracts in which they are financially interested.

sections.conclusion: It is clear from the facts in this matter that any advice we provide relating to a potential conflict of interest would relate to past conduct. Therefore, we consider your request for advice withdrawn.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for advice regarding potential conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, specifically related to Government Code section 1090.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requested guidance would relate to past conduct, which is prohibited by Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and Government Code section 1097.1(c)(2).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers. The question and conclusion were extracted from the body text. Document clearly indicates the request is withdrawn due to past conduct prohibition.

QUESTION: The requester asked for advice regarding potential conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, specifically related to Government Code section 1090.

CONCLUSION: It is clear from the facts in this matter that any advice we provide relating to a potential conflict of interest would relate to past conduct. Therefore, we consider your request for advice withdrawn.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew the advice request from Charles Grace regarding potential conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act because the Commission is statutorily prohibited from rendering advice related to past conduct in connection with conflict of interest provisions.
